story.text: The throughput fix the reviewer puts above all else: a terminal-pull gateway (next_landable_slice) that CONSUMES ready, receipt-complete slices and pulls them to main. Its guard asks 'can this slice be pulled now?' — all required receipts present, dependencies landed or in-slice, workspace binding valid, merge authority present, slice small enough for current capacity, age/churn within threshold. Directly attacks the binding constraint: 5 arcs at zero landings, 27 confirmed-but-unpulled pieces. Value: work that is judged-good actually reaches main instead of accumulating.

_note: A foreign review's finding, returned to package 'landing-throughput' and landed as proposed work through the inbound envoy seam (done-line 0059). The value gate judges the story; bdo's confirm-arc on 'epic.landing-throughput-response' queues the whole review at once. Never mutated — the fold over .ai-native/log/ is the live state (D-5).
